updates.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Sonnet 5 — New Default Model with Near-Opus Performance and 1M Token Context
updates.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 on June 30, 2026, making it the new default model for Free and Pro tiers immediately across all plans (Free, Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise, Claude Code, and the Claude Platform). The model closes the performance gap with Opus 4.8 on agentic tasks including reasoning, tool use, software coding, and knowledge work. Introductory pricing is $2/M input and $10/M output tokens through August 31, 2026, rising to $3/$15 afterward; Claude Code ships Sonnet 5 as its default with a native 1M-token context window.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic official news page and independently corroborated by TechCrunch, MacRumors, 9to5Mac, and TechTimes. Pricing figures and context window size are consistent across all sources. Performance gap vs Opus 4.8 is based on Anthropic benchmarking — independent third-party benchmark validation pending.

updates.title: Anthropic Redeploys Claude Fable 5 After US Lifts Export Controls — New Classifier Blocks Jailbreak in 99%+ of Cases
updates.summary: Claude Fable 5 returned globally on July 1, 2026 after the US Commerce Department lifted the export controls it had imposed on June 12, which forced Anthropic to suspend the model for all users worldwide. The ban was triggered by Amazon researchers discovering a jailbreak that could cause the model to identify software vulnerabilities and, in one case, write exploit code. Anthropic deployed a new cybersecurity classifier that blocks the specific jailbreak technique in over 99% of attempts; blocked requests silently fall back to Claude Opus 4.8, and Anthropic opened a HackerOne bug bounty program for new Fable 5 jailbreaks.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic's official redeployment post, corroborated by The Hacker News, Tom's Hardware, 9to5Mac, Gizmodo, TechTimes, Infosecurity Magazine, and Medianama. The 99%+ block rate is Anthropic's own self-reported figure and has not been independently audited. The June 12 export control order date and June 30 Commerce Department lift date are consistently reported across all sources.

instagram.hook: Claude's most powerful AI was banned by the US government 19 days ago. It just came back — here's the full story.

Slide 4: How Anthropic fixed it: the new 99% classifier

updates.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Science — AI Workbench for Researchers with 60+ Scientific Databases
updates.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Science in beta on June 30, an AI research workbench that integrates over 60 scientific databases and toolkits spanning genomics, proteomics, structural biology, and cheminformatics — available immediately to Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers. The platform is not a new model but runs on existing Claude models (including Opus 4.8), and every output includes an auditable history for reproducibility. Early results include a UCSF epidemiologist completing germline analyses in one-tenth the previous time, and an Allen Institute neuroscientist compressing a two-year literature review into hours. Anthropic is funding up to 50 research projects with grants of up to $30,000 in credits, with applications open through July 15, 2026.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic official news page, TechCrunch, Dataconomy, Northeastern University press release, Yahoo Tech, The Next Web, and pharmaphorum. The '60+ databases' figure, grant program terms, and early user results are consistent across sources. The comparison to OpenAI's GPT-Rosalind (launched April 2026) is noted by TechCrunch as competitive context.

updates.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom Forge First-of-Its-Kind Deal — Claude at 50% Discount for All California Government Agencies
updates.summary: Governor Gavin Newsom announced June 29 that Anthropic and California have formed a first-of-its-kind partnership making Claude available to all state agencies and local governments (cities, counties) at a 50% discounted price, along with free workforce training and Anthropic developer support. The CA DMV is using Claude to improve customer service and reduce wait times, while the Department of Healthcare Services — the largest Medicaid agency in the US — is using it for internal workflows. The deal comes amid Anthropic's public dispute with the Pentagon, which rejected the company's AI safety guardrails and signed with OpenAI instead.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via the Governor of California's official press release (gov.ca.gov), TechCrunch, The Next Web, and PYMNTS. The 'first-of-its-kind' designation and 50% discount terms come directly from the Governor's press office. The Pentagon-Anthropic dispute context is corroborated by multiple tech news sources but is background context, not part of today's announcement itself.

updates.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Apps Gateway — Self-Hosted Enterprise Control Plane for Claude Code on AWS, GCP & Azure
updates.summary: Anthropic launched the Claude apps gateway, a self-hosted control plane for enterprise Claude Code deployments on Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry. The gateway runs as a single stateless container backed by PostgreSQL and provides corporate SSO, centrally enforced usage policy, role-based access control, per-user cost attribution, and configurable daily/weekly/monthly spend caps per org, group, or user. For Bedrock or GCP deployments, inference traffic and usage data stay on the customer's infrastructure and are never sent to Anthropic.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic's official Claude blog, Claude Code docs, DevOps.com, TipRanks, and blockchain.news. Technical architecture details (stateless container, PostgreSQL backing) come directly from official documentation. Microsoft Foundry support appears in some sources alongside the claude.com docs and should be treated as confirmed. Data privacy claim is a significant enterprise selling point — verified in official docs.

updates.title: Forget Vibe-Coding Apps — People Are Now Vibe-Editing Videos in Claude Code
updates.summary: XDA Developers reports that Claude Code is expanding beyond software development into video editing, highlighted by Anthropic staffer Thariq Shihipar publicly sharing that he edited Fable 5's own launch video entirely inside Claude Code. The article explores how Claude Code's agentic architecture — reading and writing files, running terminal commands, installing dependencies, and calling external tools — makes it a general-purpose automation platform well beyond coding. The piece frames this as a 'vibe-editing' trend where non-technical creators are using AI agents for media production tasks.
updates.accuracy_notes: Published June 29, 2026 (confirmed by search metadata showing 'published June 29, 3:08 PM EDT'). The Thariq Shihipar claim is attributed to an Anthropic staff member's public share, which is independently verifiable. The broader capability claims about Claude Code file/command handling are consistent with official Claude Code documentation.

updates.title: I Paired These Chrome Extensions with Claude and My Workflow Completely Changed
updates.summary: XDA Developers writer Mahnoor Faisal shares how Chrome extensions — particularly ClaudeKarma — dramatically improved their Claude workflow by solving the usage-limit visibility problem Anthropic's native interface lacks. ClaudeKarma provides a live session gauge showing percentage of the 5-hour rate limit used, per-model usage breakdowns displayed as stacked bars, a GitHub-style usage heatmap across weeks and months, and a peak-hours countdown to help users shift heavy tasks to off-peak windows. The article addresses a widespread frustration among Claude Pro and Max subscribers who frequently hit rate limits without warning.
updates.accuracy_notes: Published June 29, 2026 (confirmed by search metadata showing 'published June 29, 8:30 AM EDT'). ClaudeKarma features described are based on the author's personal use and should be verified against the extension's current release. The '5-hour limit' and peak hours system are consistent with Anthropic's documented rate-limiting structure.
